description_long: This object consists of three stylized yellow flowers with polygonal, disk-shaped heads and distinct, angular petal-like projections around the edges. The flower heads are supported by slender, green stems that rise vertically and curve slightly, giving a natural, organic appearance. The petals are arranged radially, with each flower head displaying a symmetrical pattern of evenly spaced, geometric segments. No leaves are visible along the stems, emphasizing the minimalistic and low-poly design. The coloration is uniform, with the flower heads in a muted yellow and the stems in a medium green. The overall form suggests a wildflower or ornamental plant commonly found in urban parks or roadside plantings.
description: A cluster of three yellow, polygonal flowers with radial petals and green, slender stems. The design is minimalistic and stylized, with no visible leaves.
description_view_0: Two yellow flower heads are visible, with one partially obscured behind the other; stems are clustered closely together.
description_view_1: All three flower heads are visible, with the tallest in the back and two shorter ones in the foreground, showing the radial petal arrangement clearly.
description_view_2: The three flower heads are staggered vertically, with the lowest flower head fully visible and the others overlapping above.
description_view_3: The tallest flower head is prominent, with two shorter flower heads angled away, displaying the stems' curvature.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration around each flower head, with geometric, evenly spaced segments forming a symmetrical pattern. There is no visible overlapping or layering, and the arrangement is whorled and regular.
